frameworks.name: continuous attractor neural network (CANN)
frameworks.one_line: bump-attractor substrate for holding multiple items during WM retention

frameworks.name: short-term synaptic plasticity (STP)
frameworks.one_line: STP modulates synaptic efficacies to set relative item memory strengths

frameworks.name: multi-item working memory dynamics
frameworks.one_line: temporal dynamics of co-held items as the locus of WM storage

core_question: Can temporally coherent luminance perturbation during retention causally alter the relative memory strengths of multiple items held in human working memory?
key_assumptions: Multi-item WM retention relies on temporally structured neural dynamics
External temporally coherent input can entrain item-specific neural representations
STP-modulated CANN captures human multi-item WM behavior
manipulations: Temporally correlated luminance sequences presented during retention
Relative coherence of perturbation across competing WM items
Six experimental variants probing the manipulation
key_findings: Dynamic perturbation during retention reliably alters relative WM item strength across six experiments
Effect replicates in over 150 human subjects
CANN+STP model reproduces the empirical behavioral pattern
interpretations: Multi-item WM strengths are causally set by temporal neural dynamics during retention
STP-driven synaptic efficacy changes mediate the behavioral effect
